skill.name: developing-genkit-python
skill.description: Develop AI-powered applications using Genkit in Python. Use when the user asks about Genkit, AI agents, flows, or tools in Python, or when encountering Genkit errors, import issues, or API problems.
skill.summary: Reference skill for building AI applications with the Genkit Python SDK, including flows, tools, streaming, FastAPI integration, and evaluations.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: All 233 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation-only reference for developing Genkit Python applications. All detected patterns (shell commands, URLs, API key references, file paths) appear inside markdown code blocks and documentation text as legitimate examples for developers. No executable code, network requests, or file operations are performed by the skill itself. The skill documents GEMINI_API_KEY usage which is expected and required for Genkit Google AI development, but no credentials are exfiltrated.

content.user_title: Build AI Apps with Genkit in Python
content.value_statement: Developers often struggle with the Genkit Python SDK due to its fast-changing API and complex setup requirements. This skill provides up-to-date code patterns, error fixes, and workflow guidance for building AI-powered applications with Genkit in Python.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured and streaming text output using Genkit Python SDK
Create and run Genkit flows with tools, media parts, and embeddings
Set up FastAPI endpoints with Genkit streaming support
Define and evaluate AI model output using built-in and custom evaluators
Use Dotprompt templates for managing prompt variants and partials
Debug common Genkit errors with detailed troubleshooting guidance
limitations: Only covers the Genkit Python SDK, not the TypeScript or Node.js SDK
Requires Python 3.14 or higher and the uv package manager
Does not include deployment guidance for specific cloud platforms
Examples focus on the Google AI provider; other providers may differ

use_cases.title: Rapid Genkit Prototyping
use_cases.description: A Python developer new to Genkit can quickly scaffold a project, set up the Gemini API, and build a working AI flow with structured output in minutes.

use_cases.title: FastAPI Integration for AI Services
use_cases.description: A backend developer can expose Genkit flows as streaming HTTP endpoints using FastAPI, with built-in SSE support for real-time AI responses.

use_cases.title: Genkit Error Debugging
use_cases.description: A developer maintaining or upgrading a Genkit Python app can reference common errors, model ID issues, and import problems documented in the skill.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Genkit Setup and First Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new Genkit Python project. Create a main.py file with Google AI plugin configured and a simple flow that summarizes text. Include instructions for running with genkit start.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new Genkit Python project

prompt_templates.title: Streaming FastAPI Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a FastAPI application with Genkit that exposes a streaming chat endpoint. Use the genkit_fastapi_handler decorator and include a client-side JavaScript example using EventSource.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a streaming AI API server

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Flow Parallel Orchestration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Genkit application with three analysis flows (security, bugs, style) that run in parallel using asyncio.gather. Combine results into a single FastAPI endpoint.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a code analysis tool with parallel AI flows

prompt_templates.title: Custom Evaluator for LLM Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a Genkit evaluator that uses an LLM judge model to score output quality. Include a dataset JSON file with test cases and CLI commands to run the evaluation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up automated AI output evaluation

output_examples.input: Generate a streaming story about a robot learning to paint
output_examples.output: The AI flow generates a story one sentence at a time, displaying each chunk in real time. The complete story appears once streaming finishes, with the title and genre extracted as structured data.

output_examples.input: Analyze a Python code snippet for security issues, bugs, and style problems
output_examples.output: Three analysis flows run in parallel. Security findings list potential vulnerabilities, bug analysis flags correctness issues, and style review suggests improvements. All results combine into a single report.

best_practices: Always use ai.run_main(main()) instead of asyncio.run(main()) when running under genkit start.
Wrap all tool function parameters in a Pydantic BaseModel to avoid 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT errors.
Use model IDs with plugin prefix (e.g., googleai/gemini-flash-latest) to ensure correct routing.
anti_patterns: Using await ai.generate_stream() instead of calling it synchronously and iterating over sr.stream.
Defining tool functions with bare scalar parameters instead of a Pydantic BaseModel input type.
Using @ai.define_tool() decorator instead of the correct @ai.tool() decorator.

faq.question: What Python version does Genkit require?
faq.answer: Genkit Python requires Python 3.14 or higher. Use uv venv --python 3.14 to create a compatible virtual environment.

faq.question: How do I install Genkit and its plugins?
faq.answer: Use uv add genkit genkit-plugin-google-genai to install Genkit with the Google AI plugin. Other plugins follow the same pattern: uv add genkit-plugin-name.

faq.question: Why do I get a 400 INVALID_ARGUMENT error with tools?
faq.answer: This error occurs when tool functions use bare scalar parameters. Wrap all parameters in a Pydantic BaseModel class to provide the required object schema.

faq.question: Should I use asyncio.run or ai.run_main?
faq.answer: For long-running Genkit apps started with genkit start, use ai.run_main(main()). For one-off scripts that exit immediately, asyncio.run() is acceptable.

faq.question: How do I stream responses from a Genkit flow?
faq.answer: Use ai.generate_stream() (no await) to get a stream result, then iterate with async for chunk in sr.stream. For FastAPI, the genkit_fastapi_handler auto-streams when the client sends Accept: text/event-stream.

faq.question: How do I run Genkit evaluations?
faq.answer: Install genkit-plugin-evaluators, register evaluators with register_genkit_evaluators(ai), then run genkit eval:run datasets/my_dataset.json from the CLI. Results appear in the Dev UI.
